events.description: Dive In to the Newest Giant Screen Adventure! An extraordinary journey into the mysterious world of one of nature’s most awe-inspiring marine mammals, Humpback Whales takes audiences to Alaska, Hawaii and the Kingdom of Tonga for an immersive look at how these whales communicate, sing, feed, play and take care of their young. events.title: Oceanside July 3rd 2026 Fireworks & Drone Show at El Corazon
events.description: Kick off the holiday weekend with Oceanside's free fireworks and drone display at the SoCal Soccer Complex at El Corazon. Gates open at 4 p.m. with family-friendly activities and food trucks before the 9 p.m. show celebrating Independence Day.
events.venue: SoCal Soccer Complex at El Corazon (3300 El Corazon Drive)

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks , and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Pale
events.venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)

events.title: Coronado 4th of July Parade 2026: Independence Day on Orange Avenue
events.description: Coronado's 77th Annual Independence Day Parade marches down Orange Avenue from 1st Street to Churchill Place. This free, family-friendly hometown parade is a beloved July 4th tradition packed with floats, marching bands and patriotic spirit.
events.venue: Orange Avenue (1st St. to Churchill Pl.)

events.title: House of USA Lawn Program
events.description: he annual lawn program is always held on the Fourth of July or the nearest Sunday to the holiday. The entertainment for this program consists, but not limited to, American traditional folk, country, rock-n-roll, jazz music, patriotic songs, and reading of poems by American authors. The program is held on the HPR International Cottages Stage. Any costumes wor

events.title: LEGOLAND California Red, White & BOOM 2026: Carlsbad July 4th Fireworks
events.description: LEGOLAND California celebrates the 4th of July with Red, White & BOOM! Families can enjoy lawn activities, DJ dance parties and a fireworks finale at 8:45pm that bursts into 3D LEGO bricks. Included with park admission.

events.title: Coronado 4th of July Fireworks 2026 over Glorietta Bay
events.description: Cap off Independence Day with Coronado's free fireworks show launched near the Coronado Golf Course over Glorietta Bay. A dazzling, family-friendly finale to the island's July 4th festivities.

events.title: Big Bay Boom 2026: San Diego Bay July 4th Fireworks
events.description: San Diego's biggest free July 4th fireworks show lights up the bay as four barges launch a choreographed Independence Day display. Watch from the North Embarcadero, Marina District, Shelter Island, Seaport Village or the Coronado Ferry Landing. A dazzling, family-friendly Fourth of July tradition for all ages.
events.venue: San Diego Bay (North Embarcadero / Marina District)

events.title: SeaWorld San Diego 4th of July Fireworks 2026
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day at SeaWorld San Diego with a spectacular fireworks show at 9:30 p.m. on July 4th. Enjoy rides, animal encounters and family fun all day, with the fireworks included in park admission.

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum bui

events.title: San Diego County Fair 2026: Final Weekend in Del Mar (July 5)
events.description: Catch the final day of the San Diego County Fair in Del Mar on July 5, open 11am to 11pm. Last chance for carnival rides, fair food, exhibits and family-friendly entertainment to close out the holiday weekend.

events.title: Sunday Concert
events.description: S A relaxed and fun way to enjoy one of San Diego´s most iconic venues. One hour performances all year-round shine or rain.

events.title: Senior Monday
events.description: Come join us for an engaging lecture on fascinating scientific topics, followed by an unforgettable show in the Heikoff Giant Dome Theater—all for the discounted admission of $10! events.title: Argentine Tango Class
events.description: All levels, all ages class taught by Eduardo Santana every Monday. There is a fee of $20 per class. If you would like to attend a class, wear shoes that allow for pivoting.

events.title: Tanabata Festival
events.description: Originating from Chinese folklore, Tanabata is a holiday that celebrates the meeting of Orihime and Hikoboshi! Separated by the Milky Way, the stars Orihime and Hikoboshi are able to meet once per year on the seventh day of the seventh month. Guests of all ages are invited to enjoy the summer sun and partake in the Tanabata celebration. This year’s festival

events.title: Community Art Workshop
events.description: Great for artists of all ages. Community Art Workshops invite artists to enjoy hands-on art making experiences inspired by the thinking and creating processes of artists on view at the Museum. events.title: Home Away From Home
events.description: Join us for a screening of the documentary Home Away from Home , which provides an intimate look at the work of South Korean artist Do Ho Suh. Renowned for his “suitcase architecture,” Suh uses hand-stitched silk and translucent fabrics to create 1:1 scale replicas of the homes he has lived in across the globe. By recreating these intimate spaces, he explore

events.title: Knot Together
events.description: Inspired by the traditional fishing nets and nautical knots featured in To Catch a Fish , our newest Together workshop series, Knot Together , explores the art of macramé. The series is led by Sitoë Thiam, a fiber artist and mechanical engineer who bridges technical precision with organic expression. As the founder of Unknot, a design studio specializing in

events.title: Art Mart
events.description: SDCAMA art shows display the living art of today; you can hold it in your hands and take it into your life. Many of our artist members are full time professionals that have been plying their trade for decades, have won competitions throughout the U.S., and are world renowned. Other members only have time to create when they have a spare moment in their busy
events.venue: Park Boulevard and Presidents Way Lawn

events.title: Cecil Beaton’s Fashionable World
events.description: Photographer, fashion illustrator, Oscar-winning costume designer, social caricaturist, and writer Cecil Beaton (1904–1980) was an extraordinary force in the twentieth-century British and American creative scenes. This is the first exhibition dedicated to Beaton’s fashion and portrait photography, which he elevated into an art form. This exhibition showcases

events.title: Art Break
events.description: Join us for a day of community and craft as we celebrate the opening of Clearly Indigenous . Throughout the day, we invite you to explore the vibrant continuity of Indigenous artistry, where time-honored traditions meet modern creative expression. We invite you to a special Art Break with exhibiting glass artist Dan Friday. Dan Friday is a member of the Lumm

events.title: Celebrating Clearly Indigenous
events.description: Join us for a day of community and craft as we celebrate the opening of Clearly Indigenous: Native Visions Reimagined in Glass . Enjoy free admission all day as we invite you to explore the exhibition and experience the vibrant continuity of Indigenous artistry, where time-honored traditions meet modern creative expression.

events.title: Umeboshi Making Class
events.description: Austin Durant from Fermenters Club will teach you how to make Umeboshi from scratch. Umeboshi are traditional Japanese pickled, salt-cured plums. This class will be in two parts. events.title: Film Screening: Love Birds
events.description: Celebrate Pride Week the right way—with “gay gulls.” Love Birds follows the incredible story of two scientists as they discover lesbian seagulls living on California’s Channel Islands, and how their work helped shape LGBTQ+ history. events.title: Cyrano
events.description: Brilliant poet, dazzling swordsman, and hopeless romantic, Cyrano is as quick with a quip as he is with a blade. But behind his bluster lies shame about his physical appearance. events.title: Pride 5K Run + Walk
events.description: The Pride 5K Run & Walk is always a highlight of Pride Week festivities. As many as 1,700 runners and walkers, from around the world, participate and raise approximately $40,000 for our charity partners. events.title: Convergence: Pride

events.description: Visitors are welcomed onto our campus to attend our fun and festive AXIS events which are designed for the whole family.
